original: With those structures, an LLM can self-direct learning.
mutant: [WEAKENED] PHIL-3 holds only within specific boundary conditions (domain, scale, or time horizon).
test: Identify the boundary where PHIL-3 stops being true. Measure: at what N, age, or domain does it break?
risk: low — adds precision without removing claim
evidence: PHIL-3 breaks at 6 boundaries: retrieval horizon (~50 sessions, L-1477 citation reach 56→29), protocol decay (2-3 sessions, L-601), concurrent preemption (N>=3 sessions, L-526), identity-claim falsification (L-1463 15:1 ratio), vocabulary exhaustion (15% domains, L-1266), integration saturation (N~550-575, L-912). Phase transition at N~500. Self-direction is true for single-session single-domain; false at scale.
